topic: Why most people fail behavioral interviews — and how to fix it in one week
hook: Most people fail behavioral interviews before they even finish their first answer. Here's the one-week fix nobody talks about.
fullScript: Here's the truth: behavioral interviews don't trip people up because the questions are hard. They trip people up because most answers are painfully vague. Saying 'I'm a great team player' means nothing. Saying 'I led a cross-functional team that cut delivery time by 30%' wins offers. That's where the STAR method changes everything. Situation, Task, Action, Result. Four components that force you to tell a real story with real impact. But here's the part most career coaches skip — reading STAR examples in your head does almost nothing. You have to practice out loud. Repeatedly. Why? Because under pressure, your brain retrieves what it has rehearsed, not what it has read. One week of daily out-loud practice builds what psychologists call retrieval fluency — the ability to recall stories smoothly when nerves are high. AGE Interview Prep gives you an AI voice coach available 24/7, so you can drill real behavioral questions, hear yourself, and improve fast. Your dream job is one week of practice away. Start today.
caption: Vague answers are killing your interviews 😬 Master the STAR method + practice OUT LOUD daily — and watch everything change in just one week. 🎯 #InterviewTips #BehavioralInterview #JobSearch #CareerAdvice #STARMethod

topic: Why practicing in your head doesn't work — the neuroscience of interview prep
hook: You've read 40 interview tips articles. You know the STAR method cold. You still bombed the interview. Here's the neuroscience of why.
fullScript:
There's a cognitive phenomenon called retrieval fluency — the ease with which your brain pulls information under pressure.

Reading interview tips builds familiarity. It does not build retrieval fluency. Under the pressure of a real interview, your brain retrieves what it has practiced out loud repeatedly — not what it has read.

This is why people know the STAR method cold and still ramble when they're sitting across from a hiring panel.

The fix is not more articles. The fix is repetition under simulated pressure.

AGE Interview Prep puts you in a live AI voice interview — real-time, role-specific questions, scored responses, immediate feedback. You hear yourself stumble. You identify the weak spots. You run it again.

By session three, your answers are crisp, specific, and confident — not because you've memorized scripts, but because your brain has practiced this experience enough times to retrieve fluently under pressure.

The offer goes to whoever practiced more. Start today.

imageDirection: Split graphic with bold contrast. TOP half — red/dark section labeled 'MYTH' in large red text. Below: 'Reading interview tips prepares you for the interview.' hook: MYTH: Reading interview tips prepares you for the interview. TRUTH: Practicing out loud does. Here's the science behind why they're not the same thing.
caption:
MYTH: Reading interview prep tips gets you ready.

TRUTH: It builds familiarity. Not performance.

Here's the cognitive science:

"Retrieval fluency" = how smoothly your brain recalls information under pressure.

Reading builds recognition. Out-loud practice builds retrieval fluency.

Under the pressure of a real interview, your brain retrieves what it has practiced out loud — not what it has read in an article.

This is why people know every interview tip and still stumble when the pressure is real.

The fix: practice the conversation, not the content.

AGE Interview Prep gives you an AI voice interviewer — real questions, real pressure, scored feedback — so your brain practices the right thing.
